eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on CZ's (@cz_binance) X posts from May 8-15 reflects high uncertainty, with all outcome bins priced equally at 41%, as recent weeks consistently resolved in the 20-39 range—21 posts April 28-May 5, similar prior periods—driven by steady engagement on his "Freedom of Money" memoir promotions, Dubai book signing event, and crypto updates like the Teucrium 2x Long BNB ETF launch. This baseline activity keeps mid-range bins viable, but lack of scheduled catalysts maintains a tight race across low (<20) and high (200+) tails. Spikes could arise from macro crypto volatility, major announcements, or travel; a quiet period might favor under 40.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Warsaw be 16°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Warsaw Chopin Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Warsaw Chopin Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pl/warsaw/EPWA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight clustering in ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts for Warsaw's May 9 high temperature around 15–17°C, driven by a persistent cool northerly airflow and variable mid-level cloud cover limiting solar insolation. Over the past 48 hours, updated model runs have shown minimal spread—most members projecting daytime boundary layer temperatures in this narrow band—explaining the near-even implied probabilities amid 1–2°C uncertainty from differing cloud evolution and light winds (4 m/s). Below-average May conditions stem from a stalled upper-level trough, contrasting historical averages near 18°C; key differentiators include morning clearing potential boosting 17°C or persistent overcast favoring 15°C. IMGW updates and May 8 observations could sharpen resolution before the event.

markets.question: Seattle Mariners vs. Chicago White Sox
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Seattle Mariners and Chicago White Sox, scheduled for May 8 at 7:40PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Seattle Mariners" if the Seattle Mariners win the game.

This market will resolve to "Chicago White Sox" if the Chicago White Sox win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Seattle Mariners head to Guaranteed Rate Field for a three-game series against the Chicago White Sox starting May 8, with both clubs hovering around .500—Mariners at 16-17 (fourth in AL West) and White Sox at 15-17 (third in AL Central). Mariners momentum stems from a strong 5-1 road trip capped by a series win over the Twins, though injuries sideline INF Brendan Donovan (groin), 1B Patrick Wisdom (oblique), and OF Victor Robles (pectoral), plus reliever Matt Brash's recent side discomfort. White Sox boast a four-game win streak including a sweep of the Angels and an 8-2 victory over the Padres behind Masyn Murakami's hot bat, bolstered by Jarred Kelenic's recent call-up but hampered by absences like OF Everson Pereira and SP Jonathan Cannon. Probable pitchers remain TBD, setting up a closely contested matchup with home-field edge for Chicago.

markets.question: Will 80 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz on any day by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a daily number of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above the listed value for any date between market creation and May 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The number of daily transit calls/arrivals includes container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a daily number of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points, made within this market’s timeframe, will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects persistent uncertainty in Strait of Hormuz transits amid the 2026 crisis, where Iranian restrictions since February 28 have slashed daily vessel calls from pre-conflict averages of 100+ to single digits—recently 9-11 via U.S. "Project Freedom" escorts—as per IMF Portwatch and live trackers. This embeds a $10-20 per barrel disruption premium in Brent crude, trading near $102/bbl on May 7, while very large crude carrier (VLCC) spot rates have surged to 280 Worldscale (+460% pre-crisis) and war risk insurance to 1-5% of hull value. Key swing factors include weekly IMF data releases, ongoing U.S. naval operations, and stalled U.S.-Iran talks, with President Trump pausing escorts pending a potential deal; normalization hinges on unrestricted access concessions by May 31.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest numerical weather prediction models, including ensembles from GFS and ECMWF reflected in aggregator forecasts like Weather.com, project Jeddah's highest temperature on May 7 at around 92°F (33°C), with sunny skies and northerly winds of 15–20 mph fostering sea-breeze moderation that caps peak heating. This trader consensus—tightly clustered 19–23% implied probabilities for 32–35°C—stems from a recent shift from earlier hotter outlooks (e.g., resolved 37°C on May 2), driven by a weakening high-pressure ridge allowing cooler mid-level air advection over the Red Sea coast. Historical May averages near 36–37°C at King Abdulaziz International Airport provide context for the below-climatology outlook, though minor model spreads on afternoon convective potential introduce uncertainty; daily forecast updates from NOAA and Saudi NCM will likely sharpen resolution criteria around official airport observations.

markets.question: Will Anthony Hudson win the 2026 Michigan Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Michigan, scheduled to take place on August 4, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Michigan Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Michigan Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors self-funded businessman Perry Johnson at 60.5% to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, propelled by his unprecedented $10 million TV ad blitz launched in February, which has sustained momentum amid a crowded field. U.S. Rep. John James trails at 31%, buoyed by strong name recognition from prior Senate bids, though recent polls diverge: Emerson (April 11-13) showed a near-tie at 21%-20%, while Detroit Chamber/Glengariff (April 17-19) gave James a 37%-20% edge. Evan Space, a perennial candidate, draws 17.2% amid undecided voters and low turnout expectations; Aric Nesbitt and others lag. With filing deadlines passed and Tom Leonard's April 23 withdrawal consolidating support, upcoming debates could shift battleground dynamics in this open-seat race replacing term-limited Gov. Gretchen Whitmer.

https://www.binance.com/en/futures/xmrusdt with the chart set to “1m” (one-minute candles) on the top bar.

markets.question: Will François Asselineau be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With President Emmanuel Macron constitutionally barred from a third consecutive term, the 2027 French presidential election—first round expected in April—features a fragmented field shaped by the 2024 hung parliament and no-confidence votes destabilizing governments. Hard-left leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon announced his fourth bid on May 4, 2026, amid recent declarations from Bruno Retailleau and positioning by frontrunners like National Rally's Jordan Bardella and Édouard Philippe, who polls best against far-right opponents in runoffs. March municipal elections curbed RN advances while aiding left-wing urban gains. Qualification requires 500 parrainages from elected officials across 30 departments; traders eye summer declarations, party maneuvers, and polling shifts ahead of signature collection.

markets.question: Will Gardner Minshew start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Recent offseason acquisitions like Justin Fields (25.5%) as primary backup and veteran options Joe Flacco (28.1%) and Gardner Minshew (7.5%) reflect Kansas City's deepened QB room post-contract restructure, prioritizing insurance while prioritizing Mahomes' long-term role.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's closely contested March 2026 parliamentary elections, which produced a hung parliament with no outright majority, incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement failed to secure a coalition by mid-April, prompting President Nataša Pirc Musar to decline nominating a candidate amid impasse. Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS), finishing a close second, has since advanced center-right coalition negotiations with allies including the New Slovenia (NSi) party, announcing agreement on key guidelines as of May 5—driving trader consensus to 93% implied probability for Janša. Finalizing cabinet allocations and policy details remains pending, with a National Assembly confidence vote imminent; potential challenges include negotiation breakdowns, key defections, or unresolved disputes leading to alternatives like Jernej Vrtovec or snap elections.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

markets.question: Will there be no head of state of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

markets.question: Will Franco Colapinto be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (LOW) $264 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

This market will resolve to "Club Independiente Petrolero" if Club Independiente Petrolero win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Caracas FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on conmebol.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Caracas FC vs. Club Independiente Petrolero - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Copa Sudamericana game, scheduled for April 15 at 6:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Caracas FC hosts Club Independiente Petrolero in the Copa Sudamericana group stage opener on April 15 at Estadio Olímpico de la UCV, where home advantage could prove key amid Caracas's struggles in Liga FUTVE Apertura—sitting 11th with a 1-4-3 record, including recent draws like 1-1 versus Monagas and losses exposing defensive frailties. Independiente Petrolero, mid-table in Bolivia's Primera División with mixed results such as a recent 0-0 stalemate against Guabirá, brings solid away form but faces travel and adaptation challenges. No major injuries or suspensions reported for either side, and limited head-to-head history leaves the matchup closely contested based on trader consensus, with group positioning implications heightening stakes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid commands 48.5% implied probability in the Hart Memorial Trophy market after clinching the Art Ross Trophy with a league-leading 138 points (48 goals, 90 assists) over 82 games, capping a dominant late-season tear with hat tricks and multi-point outbursts that propelled the Edmonton Oilers into Stanley Cup Playoffs contention. Nikita Kucherov's 30.9% reflects his 130-point output driving the Tampa Bay Lightning's strong Atlantic finish and earning him the top spot in NHL.com's end-of-season Trophy Tracker voting among Professional Hockey Writers' Association members. Nathan MacKinnon's 15.5% stems from 127 points—including a league-high 53 goals—and anchoring the Colorado Avalanche's elite 55-16-11 Central Division-winning record, underscoring trader consensus on these forwards' regular-season team impact amid ongoing playoffs.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seattle be between 74-75°F on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts for Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (KSEA) indicate a high temperature near 64-66°F today, driving trader consensus toward 64-65°F at 39.5% implied probability, following yesterday's observed maximum of 57°F amid persistent marine stratus clouds and cool Puget Sound inflow. Gradual clearing and light southwest winds are expected to allow daytime heating under partly sunny skies, consistent with early May climatological averages around 64°F and recent model ensembles from NOAA showing limited warming potential due to lingering low-level moisture. Uncertainties remain in afternoon cloud evolution, with updated hourly guidance and observations through 5 PM PDT key to final resolution; historical May 7 highs range widely from 40s to 80s°F.

markets.question: Will annual inflation increase by 3.3% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about inflation over the 12-month period ending April 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

This market will resolve to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending in April 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release, which reports inflation over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g., 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders, wagering real capital, price a 40.1% implied probability for April 2026 CPI annual inflation at 3.7%, narrowly ahead of 32.7% for 3.8%, reflecting tight competition amid volatile energy dynamics following March's upside surprise to 3.3% year-over-year—driven by a 12.5% energy surge from Middle East tensions and gasoline up 21.2%. Core CPI held steady at 2.6%, underscoring headline sensitivity to oil trajectories, with Cleveland Fed nowcasting 3.56% and Trading Economics consensus at 3.3% suggesting potential moderation if geopolitical risks ease. Shelter and food trends remain key differentiators, as the May 12 Bureau of Labor Statistics release looms, poised to influence Fed funds rate path expectations.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. Recent April 29 reporting highlights intra-party criticism of Titus's Laken Riley Act vote amid calls for generational change, but her historical general election margins (52% in 2024) and weak challenger resources minimize primary risks ahead of June 9. A fragmented Republican primary field, including state Sen. Carrie Buck and others, limits GOP viability per Likely Democratic ratings from Cook and Sabato's Crystal Ball.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Vasylivka by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Vasylivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.357760° N, 37.038017° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have intensified assaults along the Vasylivka front in Zaporizhzhia Oblast's Hulyaipole direction, capturing positions near Prymorske and Pryluky in late April 2026 before Ukrainian counterattacks rolled back some gains by early May, securing supply lines to Stepnohirsk amid heavy fighting in dachas north of Plavni. Geolocated footage and open-source maps show no confirmed Russian entry into Vasylivka itself, with a Russian salient east of the main Ukrainian defenses under pressure from Ukrainian infiltrations. ISW assessments highlight Russian prioritization of this sector over other fronts, though high attrition and Ukrainian drone strikes on logistics constrain advances. No major diplomatic breakthroughs or scheduled summits loom to alter the trajectory before the market's resolution date.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the open race to replace retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler in New York's 12th Congressional District, trader consensus reflects a dead heat between Assemblymember Alex Bores at 41% and Micah Lasher at 40%, driven by massive outside spending including crypto billionaire Chris Larsen's $3.5 million super PAC infusion for Bores on May 1 amid dueling AI industry ads—for Bores from Anthropic backers, against him from OpenAI allies—while Bloomberg has poured over $4 million into Lasher. Recent internal polls show high undecideds (28%) and Jack Schlossberg leading on name recognition from his Kennedy lineage and social media presence (22%), but Bores gains among informed voters; older demographics expected to dominate June 23 primary turnout could tip the balance alongside endorsements like Hochul and Nadler for Lasher, Pelosi for Schlossberg.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Democratic Party candidate Park Chan-dae at 96.6% implied probability to win the June 3 Incheon mayoral election, propelled by recent opinion polls showing him leading incumbent People Power Party Mayor Yoo Jeong-bok by 25+ percentage points—54.9% to 29.5% in surveys from early May. Park, a three-term lawmaker and key ally of President Lee Jae-myung, capitalizes on ruling party momentum amid national approval trends favoring the Democratic Party of Korea. Yoo, seeking a third term, contends with lingering fallout from his election law violation indictment, with a first trial scheduled for May 15 that could further erode support. While Park's commanding position reflects skin-in-the-game assessments of polling stability and incumbency challenges, upset scenarios include a late scandal targeting Park, Yoo's rebound through effective campaigning in swing districts like Jemulpo-gu, or unexpectedly high conservative turnout consolidating behind the incumbent.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mike Minogue's landslide victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 27, capturing 70% of delegate votes and securing the official endorsement, has solidified his position as the clear frontrunner in trader consensus for the September 1 GOP gubernatorial primary, reflected in his 81.5% implied probability. Brian Shortsleeve narrowly cleared the 15% threshold with 15.5% to qualify for the ballot, sustaining his 26.5% odds amid calls for head-to-head debates to reach primary voters. markets.question: Will Aħwa Maltin win the most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Nicolás Maduro at 62.1% to remain Venezuela's leader by December 31, 2026, driven by chavismo's firm grip on institutions despite his January 3 U.S. military capture and ongoing New York court proceedings on narco-terrorism charges. Recent April-May developments, including Maduro's court allowance for legal defense funding, have surged his implied probability, as traders anticipate potential return or sustained official recognition amid UN credentials disputes. Acting President Delcy Rodríguez holds 24.5% on her interim Supreme Court swearing-in and U.S. sanctions relief, but faces internal purges of Maduro allies and National Assembly resistance to snap elections. Opposition leader María Corina Machado's 8.5% reflects her coalition push, though barred from office and lacking institutional leverage in the protracted transition.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in London be 8°C on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Met Office and BBC Weather forecasts for London on May 8 show minimum temperatures clustering around 10-11°C under sunny intervals or light drizzle, with light winds from the south-east, driving trader consensus toward outcomes like 9°C (22%), 10°C (21.5%), and 11°C (21%) while keeping 14°C or higher viable at 25% amid ensemble spread. Chilly nights this week—7-9°C lows on May 5-7 despite daytime highs to 18°C—highlight radiative cooling risks if skies clear overnight, versus warmer mins under persistent cloud. Historical May averages near 9°C add context, but model divergences on cloud cover and boundary layer stability create genuine uncertainty; watch tomorrow's updates from the UK Met Office for refined guidance before resolution via Wunderground observations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 25–27°C (72% combined implied probability) for Chengdu's highest temperature on May 8, aligning with the latest China Meteorological Administration (CMA) forecasts and global model ensembles like ECMWF and GFS, which project mid-20s highs amid partly cloudy skies and moderate southerly winds in the Sichuan Basin. Recent CMA updates as of May 6 indicate slight cooling from earlier-week peaks near 31°C on May 5, following cooler 24–25°C days, due to increased cloud cover from an approaching trough. Differentiating factors include model spread on afternoon convective activity and urban heat island effects, with historical early-May averages at 26.6°C adding context; expect refined odds after tonight's model runs.

markets.question: Comunicaciones FC vs. CSD Xelajú MC: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Liga Nacional Guatemala game between Comunicaciones FC and CSD Xelajú MC, scheduled for May 7 at 10:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Comunicaciones FC and CSD Xelajú MC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligagt.org. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Comunicaciones FC vs. CSD Xelajú MC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Liga Nacional Guatemala game, scheduled for May 7 at 10:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xelajú MC enters the Liga Nacional Clausura 2026 semifinals as regular-season leaders with 39 points and a flawless home record, dominating recent head-to-heads by winning the last five encounters, including a 3-1 victory at Comunicaciones in March. Comunicaciones, fourth with 35 points, advanced past defending champions Antigua GFC in quarters but struggles against Xelajú, losing their past five meetings amid mixed recent form (two wins in last five). Xelajú's away resilience bolsters trader consensus, though fresh goalkeeper concerns—Nery Lobos' potential calf rupture forcing juvenile Estuardo Chang into quarters, plus absences like Rubén Silva and long-term defender Gerardo Gordillo—could test depth. First leg Thursday at Comunicaciones' Cementos Progreso offers home edge; second leg Sunday at Mario Camposeco. Key figures: Xelajú's Jesús López, Comunicaciones' Dairon Reyes.

markets.question: Will Matteo Berrettini win the 2026 Men's French Open?
markets.description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carlos Alcaraz's April 24 withdrawal from Roland Garros due to a right wrist injury—ending his bid for a three-peat as defending champion—has vaulted Jannik Sinner to 70.5% trader consensus as the top seed and world No. 1 favorite on clay. Sinner's red-hot clay-court form, including a dominant Madrid Open title over Alexander Zverev last week and 28 straight Masters 1000 wins, underscores his baseline power and improved sliding, positioning him for a career Grand Slam. Zverev (6.5%) follows as the next strongest clay contender with strong surface history, while Novak Djokovic's lingering shoulder injury caps his odds at 5.1%, leaving the draw wide open for Sinner barring upsets from dark horses like Arthur Fils amid a depleted field.

markets.question: Taiwanese Premier Cho Jung-tai out by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Cho Jung-tai ceases to be Premier of Taiwan for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Cho Jung-tai's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Cho Jung-tai and the government of Taiwan; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Taiwan's Premier Cho Jung-tai continues to lead the Executive Yuan amid persistent legislative gridlock with the opposition-majority Legislative Yuan, controlled by the KMT and TPP since the 2024 elections. Key tensions stem from Cho's repeated refusals to countersign opposition-passed bills, including revenue-sharing amendments in December 2025 and further legislation in March 2026, prompting censure motions that advanced but stalled short of a no-confidence vote. Such a vote, needing one-quarter of legislators to propose and a simple majority to pass, could force Cho's removal, cabinet reshuffle, or even dissolution—yet opposition leaders have held back amid mutual accusations of constitutional overreach. With no major escalations in the past 30 days and November 2026 local elections approaching, trader sentiment hinges on potential budget battles or political realignments that could trigger a leadership change.

events.title: West Ham United FC vs. Arsenal FC - Halftime Result
events.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between West Ham United FC and Arsenal FC, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 11:30 AM ET: This event contains halftime result markets for home, draw, and away outcomes within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices West Ham United FC, Draw, and Arsenal FC at 49.5¢ each for the halftime result, underscoring a fiercely competitive London derby where first-half caution prevails amid late-season stakes. West Ham, battling mid-table security with a gritty 2-1 home win over Everton on April 25 and a resilient 0-0 draw at Crystal Palace days earlier, leverage London Stadium's fortress-like atmosphere and solid home form against goal-shy opening periods. Title frontrunners Arsenal, who reclaimed Premier League summit via a narrow 1-0 victory versus Newcastle on the same weekend, face injury headwinds including Bukayo Saka's ankle issue, Martin Ødegaard's knee problem, and doubts over Declan Rice and Jurrien Timber, tempering their attacking fluency early. Head-to-head history shows tight first halves, amplifying the even-market dynamics.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Metamask's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will another result occur for the next governing coalition of New Zealand?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

eventMetadata.context_description: Somaliland's House of Elders (Guurti) approved a 27-month extension of parliamentary and local council terms on April 28, 2026, postponing elections originally slated for May 2026 to March 2027 and boosting trader consensus to 61% on no vote before 2027 amid concerns over voter registration and logistical readiness. President Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi Irro of Waddani, elected in 2024, consulted the Guurti per constitutional requirements but could not avert the delay, positioning his party as the 32.5% favorite if polls proceed due to incumbency advantages from recent presidential and party status victories over Kulmiye and others. UCID trails at 5.1% and Kulmiye at 1.5%, reflecting diminished momentum post-2024 contests, though candidates have protested the extension as unconstitutional.

markets.question: Will London have less than 5mm of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in mm at Heathrow (London Airport) in May, 2026, according to the Met Office.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be the Met Office, specifically the figure for May 2026 under "rain mm" at the https://www.metoffice.gov.uk/pub/data/weather/uk/climate/stationdata/heathrowdata.txt link once the Provisional figure for the whole month of May 2026 is released.

If the relevant data is not available by July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after Provisional data is released for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will David Njoku play for Minnesota Vikings in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team David Njoku officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

If David Njoku is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Free agent tight end David Njoku remains unsigned after the 2026 NFL Draft, fueling trader consensus with Los Angeles Chargers at 51% implied probability following his recent visit amid their need for a reliable target to complement Justin Herbert's offense. Carolina Panthers trail closely at 44.8% as they skipped tight end selections, leaving Tommy Tremble and Ja'Tavion Sanders as unproven options, while Cincinnati Bengals (44.5%) eye upgrades beyond Mike Gesicki's inconsistency. Arizona Cardinals, Indianapolis Colts, and Atlanta Falcons hover around 43-44% due to similar depth issues and cap flexibility post-draft. Njoku's February announcement ruling out a Cleveland Browns return—where they signed a veteran replacement and drafted a successor—combined with his age-30 injury history, keeps the market tightly bunched among 10+ competitive landing spots despite his Pro Bowl receiving prowess.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Analilia Mejia's 20-25% margin of victory at 96.9% implied probability, reflecting final certified results released May 4 showing her 60.18% to Joe Hathaway's 39.36%—a 20.82-point win in the NJ-11 special election to replace Gov. Mikie Sherrill. This Democratic hold in the safely blue district exceeded 2024 presidential baselines by about 12 points, driven by Mejia's strong performance in diverse towns like Dover and Bloomfield, flipping 13 Trump-won precincts amid high special election turnout of 136,000 votes. Scenarios to shift odds include rare provisional ballot additions, overseas/military tallies, or a requested recount, though the landslide makes challenges unlikely before market resolution.

markets.question: Israel strike on Yemen by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Yemeni soil or any official Yemen embassy or consulate by the listed date, 11:59 PM Israeli local time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Yemen ground territory or any official Yemen embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Yemen soil is hit by an Israeli missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Yemen territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli airstrikes targeted Houthi sites in Yemen on May 6 using 20 jets, retaliating for a ballistic missile strike near Tel Aviv's Ben Gurion Airport that killed one and injured 35—the first successful Houthi hit there. This exchange follows Houthi resumption of attacks on Israel starting March 28, after a 2025 Gaza ceasefire pause, amid coordinated actions with Iran's IRGC and Hezbollah under the "axis of resistance." Yemen's Houthis threaten intensified missile and drone barrages, potential Red Sea disruptions via Bab al-Mandab Strait closure, and escalation if Israel or the US targets Iran. Traders weigh ongoing multi-front tensions, diplomatic signals, and US coordination for further military action or de-escalation.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 72.5% implied probability against the U.S. enacting a comprehensive AI safety bill before 2027, driven by Congress's stalled progress on broad regulation amid partisan disagreements over regulatory stringency. The Trump administration's March 20 National Policy Framework called for light-touch, sector-specific AI measures and state law preemption, but this advisory document has not advanced to binding legislation. Late April bipartisan Senate bills advanced narrow protections, such as restrictions on children's AI chatbot access, yet broader safety standards like risk assessments or model transparency lack committee advancement or floor votes. With the 119th Congress facing lame-duck session pressures and states enacting over 25 AI laws in 2026, federal passage faces significant procedural hurdles.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

markets.question: Will SpaceX raise less than $40B in its IPO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total gross proceeds raised by SpaceX in its initial public offering (IPO) at the time of pricing.

The amount raised is defined as the aggregate dollar value of shares sold at the final offering price at the time of IPO pricing, as disclosed in the final prospectus filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares sold pursuant to any overallotment option (greenshoe) will not be considered.

Secondary sales of existing shares by current shareholders will be included only to the extent that they are part of the IPO offering at pricing and reflected in the total gross proceeds. Private secondary transactions conducted outside of the IPO will not be considered.

If the total proceeds fall exactly on a boundary between two ranges, this market will resolve to the higher range.

If SpaceX does not complete an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The primary resolution source will be the final prospectus filed with the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 50-60 billion dollar IPO raise for SpaceX at 40.4% implied probability, reflecting skepticism toward the company's April confidential S-1 filing targeting up to 75 billion dollars at a 1.75 trillion dollar valuation despite strong Starlink revenue growth and Starship milestones. This positioning stems from recent analyst pushback, including IPO expert Jay Ritter's bearish stance on the lofty price-to-sales multiple exceeding 40x, echoing underperformance in historical mega-IPOs like Alibaba. The prior December 2025 tender offer at 800 billion dollars valuation provides a more grounded benchmark, while governance risks from Elon Musk's super-voting control add caution. Roadshow begins June 8, with trading eyed for late June.

This market will resolve to according to the candidate who wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election currently scheduled for November 3, 2026.

If the results of the election are not confirmed by July 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on official certification.

Tom Steyer remains close behind, bolstered by fresh progressive endorsements like Sierra Club and Our Revolution, alongside aggressive attack ads targeting Becerra amid fiery debates that highlighted affordability and crime. While Republicans Steve Hilton and Chad Bianco poll competitively among likely voters, the market discounts their paths due to California's deep Democratic lean and historical top-two dynamics favoring the party. Shifts could arise from final debate performances, turnout in battleground areas, or late endorsements before early voting peaks.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The U.S. unemployment rate remained stable at 4.3% in March 2026, per Bureau of Labor Statistics data released April 3, as nonfarm payrolls rose 178,000—beating subdued expectations—driven by gains in health care and construction, though federal government jobs declined. Job openings eased to 4.1% in recent JOLTS data, signaling gradual labor market cooling amid moderating GDP growth forecasts around 2% for the year. FOMC March projections point to a median 4.4% rate by Q4 2026, matching Philadelphia Fed survey estimates of 4.5%, with trader sentiment balancing Fed easing against persistent inflation risks. Key catalysts include tomorrow's April employment report (May 8) and June FOMC projections, which could shift peak expectations for the year.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Wellington be 20°C on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Wellington Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Wellington Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/nz/wellington/NZWN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors a highest temperature of 19°C in Wellington on May 7 (99.9% implied probability), driven by MetService's latest forecast models projecting a peak of 18–19°C at Wellington International Airport amid persistent cloud cover and northerly winds gusting 20–25 knots. Morning observations confirm a cool start near 15°C with 90% humidity, limiting solar heating as showers develop later, aligning with ensemble model runs updated May 6 showing no significant warm air advection. This positioning reflects historical May baselines of 14–16°C highs, nudged slightly higher by steering patterns. Realistic challenges include mid-afternoon clearing skies enabling brief solar insolation spikes to 20°C+ or early heavy rain suppressing to 18°C, though official hourly updates this afternoon will sharpen resolution.

markets.question: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the September 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's September 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for September 15-16, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their September meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 82% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the September 15-16, 2026 FOMC meeting with press conference, driven by resurgent inflation pressures and a resilient labor market supporting the current 3.50%-3.75% federal funds target range, unchanged for a third straight meeting in April. March 2026 CPI surged to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs, tempering rate-cut bets despite the March dot plot's median end-2026 projection of 3.4%. markets.question: Will Manchester City FC vs. Brentford FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 9, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
